title: Cultural Diversity in the Workplace
description: For questions 17 – 24, read the text below. Use the word given in capitals at the end of some of the lines to form a word that fits in the gap in the same line. There is an example at the beginning (0).

content.text: Embracing (0) diversity in the workplace has become a priority for many organisations around the world. Teams made up of people from different (17) cultural backgrounds bring a wider range of perspectives and ideas. This can lead to greater (18) creativity and more effective problem-solving. Research shows that (19) inclusive companies tend to be more (20) innovative and better able to serve customers from a range of backgrounds.||However, creating a truly (21) welcoming workplace takes effort. Employers must address (22) inequality in hiring and promotion, and provide (23) training to help staff understand and appreciate different cultural norms. When done (24) effectively, diversity initiatives benefit not just individuals but the organisation as a whole.

example.text: Embracing (0) DIVERSITY in the workplace has become a priority for many organisations around the world. (DIVERSE)

questions.explanation: The adjective 'cultural' is formed from 'culture' by adding the suffix '-al'.
The noun 'creativity' is formed from 'create' by replacing '-e' with '-ivity'.
The adjective 'inclusive' is formed from 'include' by replacing '-de' with '-sive'.
The adjective 'innovative' is formed from 'innovate' by adding the suffix '-ive'.
The adjective/gerund 'welcoming' is formed from 'welcome' by adding the suffix '-ing'.
The noun 'inequality' is formed from 'equal' by adding the prefix 'in-' and the suffix '-ity'.
The noun/gerund 'training' is formed from 'train' by adding the suffix '-ing'.
The adverb 'effectively' is formed from 'effect' via 'effective' + the suffix '-ly'.
